feat: add segm support to synthetic dataset & benchmark coversion (#781)

- `draw_synthetic_shape` now returns `(img, polygon)`, ensuring the COCO polygon is generated from the same geometry used to render the shape (rendering and annotation remain synchronized). Documented as a breaking return-type change.

- `generate_synthetic_sample` stores polygons in `detections.data["polygons"]` without changing the public function signature. Fixed a critical bug by reshaping polygons with `.reshape(-1, 2)` after `np.asarray(polygon)` to prevent indexing errors when polygons are provided as flat lists.

- `generate_coco_dataset` adds `with_segmentation=False` to optionally produce segmentation annotations. Dataset splitting was corrected so the final split absorbs rounding remainders, preventing silent image drops.

- COCO export logic unified into `_write_coco_json(..., with_segmentation)` replacing two previous helpers. The function now computes annotation area from polygons when segmentation is enabled and raises `ValueError` if `file_paths` and `detections_list` lengths differ to avoid silent truncation.

- Added `_calculate_polygon_area` helper for computing COCO polygon areas.

- Added `synthetic_shape_segmentation_dataset_dir` pytest fixture for segmentation dataset generation.

- Reorganized `test_synthetic.py` into five focused test classes (39 tests) and expanded coverage for polygon area computation, shape edge cases, polygon–bbox consistency, dataset split correctness, and COCO writer validation (including mismatched inputs, missing polygons, and empty polygon fallback). Fixed invalid `>= pytest.approx(...)` comparisons.

- Updated documentation and comments: completed `generate_synthetic_sample` docstrings, added a Raises section to `_write_coco_json`, and clarified triangle geometry comments.

- Rewrote the segmentation convergence benchmark to use the current training stack (`RFDETRSegNanoConfig`, `SegmentationTrainConfig`, `RFDETRModule`, `build_trainer`), removing legacy engine references. Training now reads `num_classes` from dataset JSON, runs for 5 epochs, and validates convergence with bbox `mAP ≥ 20%` and segmentation `mAP ≥ 5%`.

- Standardized benchmark/tests: renamed `test_coco_inference.py` → `test_inference_coco.py` and `test_synthetic_convergence.py` → `test_training_synthetic.py`. COCO inference benchmarks now cap `num_workers` at 4, disable `compute_val_loss`, and reduce `det-nano` sample size to 1000.

---------

BREAKING: remove target from LoseTokens, convert OnSelf costs to rolled_costs (#30)

Remove the `target` field from `CardEffectKind::LoseTokens` — LoseTokens
always implicitly targets the opponent. Add `card_value: Option<i64>` to
`ConcreteEffect` for transparent cost calculation.

Convert all LoseTokens(OnSelf) cost patterns across 5 gathering disciplines
(mining, woodcutting, crafting, fishing, herbalism) to use the rolled_costs
model on benefit effects. Each discipline now computes a card_value from its
benefit effects and stores costs as percentages of that value.

Key changes:
- types.rs: Remove target from LoseTokens variant; add card_value to ConcreteEffect
- game_state.rs: Rewrite extract_gathering_costs_from_effects to read rolled_costs;
  update check_and_deduct_costs/preview_costs to use card_value
- combat.rs: Update apply_card_effects to determine LoseTokens target implicitly
- mining.rs: Create 3 new GainTokens-with-costs templates; update all card compositions
- woodcutting.rs: Add compute/apply helpers; rewrite all 12 card compositions
- crafting.rs: Add compute/apply helpers; update 3 card compositions
- fishing.rs: Add compute/apply helpers; rewrite all 12 card compositions
- herbalism.rs: Add compute/apply helpers; rewrite all 9 card compositions
- action/mod.rs: Update 3 extract_gathering_costs_from_effects call sites

Fix tvOS: split device/simulator native builds to match iOS pattern (#3561)

* Fix tvOS: split device/simulator native builds to match iOS pattern

The tvOS native build was combining device and simulator binaries into a
single fat framework at output/native/tvos/, while the NuGet packaging
only included runtimes/tvos/native/ — missing runtimes/tvossimulator/.
This caused linker errors when building for tvOS simulator.

Changes:
- native/tvos/build.cake: Split output into tvos/ and tvossimulator/
  directories, add ios_use_simulator flag, pass SKIA_PLATFORM property
- SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.tvOS.csproj: Add tvossimulator PackageFile
- HarfBuzzSharp.NativeAssets.tvOS.csproj: Add tvossimulator PackageFile

This mirrors the proven iOS build pattern that already correctly splits
device (ios/) and simulator (iossimulator/) native assets.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix tvOS GnNinja to use min_tvos_version instead of min_ios_version

The tvOS build was incorrectly passing min_ios_version to GnNinja, which set
the iOS deployment target but left the tvOS deployment target at Skia's default
of 9.0. This changes it to min_tvos_version so the -mtvos-version-min flag is
set correctly to 11.0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: fallback through all LRC candidates on fetch failure

Previously, only the first synced candidate was tried. If its LRC file
was missing (404) or had invalid path chars like null bytes (400), the
entire repo was skipped.

Now iterates all candidates (synced first, preserving order) until one
succeeds. Skips paths containing null bytes. Only gives up when all
candidates fail.

Recovers ~24% of repos that were previously lost.

* chore: add rollback-release GitHub Actions workflow

Manual workflow_dispatch that rolls back the VPS to any previously
released image version. Accepts a version string (e.g. 0.12.4 or v0.12.4),
strips the leading 'v' to match the Docker image tag convention, pulls

deploy/rollback races. Guarded to main branch only.

commit.message:
FLTKContextManager: create real shared GLX inner contexts to fix scene_texture crash (#380)

* Initial plan

* FLTKContextManager: create real shared GLX inner contexts for proper GL isolation

Fixes _mesa_ReadPixels crash in SoSceneTexture2P::updatePBuffer when the
Rendering/scene_texture demo is shown in the obol_viewer System GL panel.

Root cause: SoOffscreenRenderer sets SORTED_OBJECT_BLEND transparency which
forces updatePBuffer (not updateFrameBuffer). updatePBuffer needs real GL
context isolation — it assumes makeContextCurrent switches to a fresh inner
context so that glReadPixels sees only tmpFbo, not the outer renderer's FBO.
The old FLTKContextManager shared one GL context for everything, so the outer
FBO binding leaked into the inner "context", causing the Mesa crash.

Fix (X11/GLX): ensureWindow() now records mainGLCtx_ and mainDrawable_ after
the first make_current(). createOffscreenContext() creates a real GLXContext
via glXCreateContext(dpy, vi, mainGLCtx_, True) — sharing textures/VBOs with
the FLTK context but having isolated GL state. makeContextCurrent() uses
glXMakeCurrent() to actually switch; restorePreviousContext() switches back.
Windows/macOS fall back to the original single-context approach (unchanged).

commit.message:
Fix falsy enum values returning None instead of name

AlarmReason.NO_ALARM has value 0, making it falsy in Python. The
_enum_to_lowercase function used 'if enum_value' which incorrectly
returned None for NO_ALARM. Changed to 'if enum_value is not None'
to properly handle zero-valued Flag enums.

This caused battery_monitor alarm and dc_energy_meter alarm to show
as 'unknown' instead of 'no_alarm' in Home Assistant.

Fix Slice shape inference crashes with SIGABRT on empty dimensions (#7738)

Fix #7735 

This pull request addresses issues with the ONNX Slice operator when
handling empty dimensions, ensuring correct and safe shape inference in
these edge cases. The main focus is to prevent undefined behavior and
ensure that slicing on empty dimensions consistently produces outputs
with zero length, regardless of the step direction. Additional
regression tests are included to verify these fixes, and several test
data files are updated to reflect the new behavior.

**Slice operator improvements for empty dimensions:**

* Updated the `processSliceInputs` function in `defs.cc` to
short-circuit and produce a zero-length output when the input dimension
size is zero, avoiding invalid clamp bounds and potential undefined
behavior.
* Modified shape inference logic in `old.cc` to detect empty dimensions
and set the output dimension value to zero, both for standard and
expanded shape inference code paths.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-5e200991755406044e2dbb2449683bb5c6d77583d1459f0065a756813dd1b59bR2635-R2645)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-5e200991755406044e2dbb2449683bb5c6d77583d1459f0065a756813dd1b59bR6329-R6335)

**Testing and regression coverage:**

* Added two new tests in `shape_inference_test.py` to verify that
slicing on empty dimensions with both positive and negative steps
produces the correct output shape, preventing regression of issue #7735.

**Test data updates:**

* Updated several `output_0.pb` binary test data files under
`onnx/backend/test/data/node/` (including attention, gelu,
hammingwindow, and lppool tests) to align with the corrected Slice
operator behavior on empty dimensions.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-875055c843db41ea7d579c8bd29201e6f60bb4175ac96f840bdd27d488866198L1-R7)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-d6fe5631baa8b6cb320c03143f797abe3dce4ddf5363fa9cfe71cc12bc31ac53L2-R3)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-3275d5e3517a0e95967de3c46a75b02104980f2566f1a0cbefa386ea8e02f312L1-R3)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-537fe44987ef880d522c9c88283070745874abc990951f49eff8dccc35630af1L2-R2)
[[5]](diffhunk://#diff-3d57e2f0cf63b32349f6619a2f61e90d4a4aa4fdac9a794af6bfb00c97fe9f8eL1-R2)

These changes collectively improve the robustness of ONNX shape
inference and operator correctness when handling tensors with empty
dimensions.
